address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

summary.p: A collection of five Hurúfi and Súfi tracts, manuscript, printed and lithographed, bound together in one volume. They are as follows:
(1) The Discourses (Maqálát) of Hájjí Bektash in Turkish. A MS. of 20 ff. of 20.3 x 13.5 cm and 19 ll., good naskh with rubrications, no colophon, date or note of acquisition.
(2) The "Interpreter of the Nations" (Tarjumánu'l-Umam) by Ibn Sadri'd-Din of Shirwán. The preface only is in Arabic, the remainder of the text in Turkish. Begins, after the doxology:

و بعد فهذا مختصر في بيان مقالات اهل العالم والمذاهب المختلفة لطوايف الأمم اوردتها على سبيل الاجمال مجتنبا عن التطويل والاملال أمرنى به من لا يسعني الا موافقته لانه أحاط بی نعمه وملاطفته اعنى الوزير الأعظم والدستور الأفخم الذى عم إحسانه وفشا، حضرت محمد پاشا، اللهم اجعل شموس دولته على فلك الوزارة مضيئة وبدور نفوس ساير الوزراء من انوار تدبّره مستضيلة ورأيته على مقدمة وعشرة أبواب وخاتمة

Ch. 1 (f. 2a) deals with the Mu'tazila; ch. II (f. 4) with the Khawárij; ch. III (f. 5) with the Shí'a; ch. IV (f. 9b) with the different varieties of the Maláḥida, or heretics of the Ismá'ílí school; ch. V (f. 11) with the Karrámiyya; ch. VI (f. 11) with the Mushabbiha or Anthropomorphists; ch. VII (f. 11) with the Murji'a; ch. VIII (f. 12) with the Najjáriyya; ch. IX (f. 12) with the Determinists or partisans of Predestination (Jabariyya); ch. X (f. 12) with the heretical Súfís (Maláḥidatu's-Súfiyya), amongst whom, says the author, are included most of the Mevlevi (Mawlawi), Gulshaní and Bektáshí dervishes. The Conclusion (Khátima) deals with the six Firaq-i-Nájiya, or groups which shall find salvation, viz. the usual four orthodox sects together with the Sufyánís and the Thawrís. Ff. 15 of 19 x 14 cm and 20 ll.; good naskh with rubrications; no colophon, date, or note of acquisition.

(3) A commentary on the qasída of the old Turkish mystical poet Yúnus Imré by Misrí Efendi, lithographed in 1268/1851-2, without indication of place, and given to me by Dr Ridá Tawfiq in August, 1909. Pp. 17 of 20 x 12.5 cm and 22 ll.; fair naskh, fully pointed. Concerning Yúnus Imré see E. J. W. Gibb's History of Ottoman Poetry, vol. 1, pp. 164-175, and a very elaborate and scholarly study in Turkish by Köprülü-záde Muhammad Fu'ád, Professor of the History of Turkish Literature in the University of Constantinople, in his work entitled Ilk Mutasawwiflar ("the First Súfís"), published at Constantinople in 1918. The second part of this book (pp. 205-394) is almost wholly devoted to this old mystic.

(4) Another manuscript copy of the 'Ishq-náma of Firishta-záda, but containing only the first seven of the thirty-two chapters into which the work is divided. Ff. 42 of 18.5 x 13 cm and 14 ll.; fair Turkish riq'a hand with rubrications; no colophon, date, or note of acquisition.

(5) A printed edition of the Turkish prose and verse works of Vírání (Wírání) Bábá, without indication of date or place of production, only a final note that it was produced for the "salvation-finding group of the Bektáshí Path":
تمت الرسالة المرغوبة ويرانى بابا بنده آل عبا سلاطین گروه ناجيه طريق بكتاشيه قدس الله سرهم تمام شد
Pp. 98 of 18 x 12.5 cm and 19 ll.

msItem.title: Maqálát | Hacı Bektaş
Discourses
msItem.note: MS. of 20 ff. of 20.3 x 13.5 cm and 19 lines, good naskh with rubrications; no colophon, date or note of acquisition.

author.persName: Shirwānī, Muḥammad al-Amīn ibn Ṣadr al-Dīn, d. 1626 or 7
شیروانی، محمد الامین بن صدر الدین

msItem.title: Tarjumān al-Umam
Interpreter of the Nations
msItem.note: Preface only in Arabic, remainder in Turkish; ten chapters and a conclusion, covering the Mu'tazila, Khawárij, Shí'a, Maláḥida, Karrámiyya, Mushabbiha, Murji'a, Najjáriyya, Jabariyya and heretical Súfís (including Mevlevi, Gulshaní and Bektáshí dervishes), with a Khátima on the six Firaq-i-Nájiya. MS. of 15 ff. of 19 x 14 cm and 20 lines, good naskh with rubrications; no colophon, date or note of acquisition.
msItem.incipit:
و بعد فهذا مختصر في بيان مقالات اهل العالم والمذاهب المختلفة لطوايف الأمم اوردتها
على سبيل الاجمال مجتنبا عن التطويل والاملال أمرنى به من لا يسعني الا موافقته لانه أحاط
بی نعمه وملاطفته اعنى الوزير الأعظم والدستور الأفخم الذى عم إحسانه وفشا، حضرت محمد
پاشا، اللهم اجعل شموس دولته على فلك الوزارة مضيئة وبدور نفوس ساير الوزراء من انوار تدبّره
مستضيلة ورأيته على مقدمة وعشرة أبواب وخاتمة
msItem.textLang: The preface only is in Arabic, the remainder of the text in Turkish

msItem.title: Commentary on the qasída of Yunüs, Emre, -1320?
msItem.note: Lithographed in 1268/1851-2, no place stated; given to Browne by Dr Ridá Tawfiq in August, 1909. Pp. 17 of 20 x 12.5 cm and 22 lines; fair naskh, fully pointed.

msItem.title: 'Ishq-náma
msItem.note: Another manuscript copy, containing only the first 7 of the 32 chapters. Ff. 42 of 18.5 x 13 cm and 14 lines; fair Turkish riq'a hand with rubrications; no colophon, date or note of acquisition.

msItem.title: Turkish prose and verse works
msItem.note: Printed edition, no date or place of production stated; a final note describes it as produced for the "salvation-finding group of the Bektáshí Path". Pp. 98 of 18 x 12.5 cm and 19 lines.
msItem.explicit: تمت الرسالة المرغوبة ويرانى بابا بنده آل عبا سلاطین گروه ناجيه طريق بكتاشيه قدس الله سرهم تمام شد

supportDesc.support: Paper; item 3 lithographed and item 5 printed rather than manuscript.
supportDesc.extent: Item 1: 20 ff. of 20.3 x 13.5 cm, 19 lines. Item 2: 15 ff. of 19 x 14 cm, 20 lines. Item 3: 17 pp. of 20 x 12.5 cm, 22 lines. Item 4: 42 ff. of 18.5 x 13 cm, 14 lines. Item 5: 98 pp. of 18 x 12.5 cm, 19 lines.

layout.p: 19, 20, 22 and 14 lines respectively for items 1, 2, 3 and 4; 19 lines for item 5.

handDesc.handNote: Items 1-3: good/fair naskh with rubrications. Item 4: fair Turkish riq'a hand with rubrications.

origin.origDate: n.d.: none of the five items bears a colophon or date.

history.provenance: No colophon, date, or note of acquisition for items 1, 2, 4 or 5; item 3 was given to Browne by Dr Ridá Tawfiq in August, 1909.
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: Summary, physical description and provenance copied from R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions. Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Claude (auto-upgrade) Auto-upgraded on the model of D_13.xml: flattened the existing single msPart/msItem (item 2) into 5 msItems built from the summary, keeping item 2's existing author key/incipit/textLang as found. The existing msPart's physDesc (98 ff., riq'a hand) actually belonged to item 5, not item 2, so per-item extent/hand figures were rebuilt from the summary instead — worth a cataloguer's check. Kept the existing LCSH keywords. Author keys left blank except item 2 (no existing Fihrist key for Hájjí Bektash, Misrí Efendi, Firishta-záda or Vírání Bábá); idno suffix "(9)" dropped as with D_2. Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer.
